title: The South Indian Bank Ltd vs Sri J E Castelino on 2 March, 2017
body: The South Indian Bank Ltd vs Sri J E Castelino on 2 March, 2017 Author: B.Sreenivase Gowda Bench: B.Sreenivase Gowda 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU DATED THIS THE 2ND DAY OF MARCH, 2017 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE B.SREENIVASE GOWDA R.S.A. NO.1935/2016 [MON] BETWEEN: THE SOUTH INDIAN BANK LTD., THE BANKING CO., REGISTERED UNDER THE BANKING COMPANIES ACT, HAVING A REGISTERED OFFICE AT TRICHUR, KERALA STATE AND BRANCH OFFICE AT HAMPANKATTA, MANGALORE-575 001. REPRESENTED BY ITS REGIONAL OFFICE, BANGALORE, AGM, MR. T. P. JOSEPH, NO.61, SIB ARCADE, COXTOWN, WHEELERS ROAD, BENGALORE - 560 005. ... APPELLANT (By SRI. ANANTHA KUMAR M, ADV.) AND: SRI. J. E. CASTELINO, S/O. LATE B. M. CASTELINO, AGED ABOUT 58 YEARS, RESIDING AT CARMEL VILLA, KULSHEKAR, MANGALORE-575 003. ... RESPONDENT (By SRI. PUNDIKAI ISHWARA BHAT, ADV.) 2 THIS RSA IS FILED U/S. 100 OF CPC AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT & DECREE DATED 21.07.2016 PASSED IN R.A.NO. 17/2013 ON THE FILE OF THE III ADDITIONAL SENIOR CIVIL JUDGE & JMFC, MANGALURU, D.K., DISMISSING THE APPEAL AND CONFIRMING THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED 21.11.2012 PASSED IN OS NO. 648/2009 ON THE FILE OF THE I ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE & JMFC., MANGALORE. THIS APPEAL COMING ON FOR ORDERS, THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: JUDGMENT This is defendant's second appeal challenging the concurrent judgments and decrees of the Courts below, whereby suit of the plaintiff has been decreed by the trial Court and it was confirmed by the first appellate Court by dismissing the appeal preferred by the defendant. 2. Heard the learned Counsel appearing for the parties. Perused the judgments and decrees of the Courts below. 3 3. Learned Counsel for the appellant (defendant - Bank) submits, in the letter Ex.D.1 dt. 28-05-2004 addressed by the plaintiff to the defendant - Bank, plaintiff has clearly stated that one time settlement was allowed by the Bank to settle the account of arrears of Rs.9,00,000/- by 31-03-2004. Accordingly, the plaintiff had paid the said amount and there was no excess payment as contended by the plaintiff. The trial Court without considering this material document has committed an error in decreeing the suit of the plaintiff by directing the defendant to return the excess payment amounting to Rs.1,43,168/- to the plaintiff with interest. The learned Counsel submits, on an earlier occasion, Bank filed a suit in O.S.No.117/2000 against the respondent for recovery of loan due to the Bank and it was decreed. As there was error in calculating the decreetal amount, the Bank had made an application for amendment of the judgment by correcting the 4 judgment and decree. During the pendency of the application, since the defendant(plaintiff in the present suit) paid a sum of Rs.9,00,000/-, the Bank had withdrawn the application as not pressed and it was 1½ years thereafter, the respondent has brought the present suit against the appellant for recovery of money on the ground that he had paid excess amount. The Courts below without considering the same, have committed an error in decreeing his suit and directing the Bank to refund the alleged excess amount to the plaintiff. Therefore, learned Counsel prays for admitting the appeal for consideration of substantial questions of law urged in the appeal. 4. Per contra, the learned Counsel appearing for the plaintiff - respondent herein submits, there is no illegality or infirmity in the judgment and decree passed by the Courts below, warranting interference of this 5 Court and he submits further that there is no substantial question of law which arises for consideration and therefore, he prays for dismissal of the appeal. 5. The case of the plaintiff was, he availed certain loan from the appellant - Bank and on account of financial problems, he could not repay the said loan as agreed upon. Therefore, the Bank had filed a suit in O. S.117 /2000 against him before the Principal Civil Judge, Mangalore for recovering the loan amount and it was decreed for a sum of Rs.3,91,328/- with interest at 10% p.a. Pursuant to the decree passed by the Court in O. S.117 /2000, plaintiff had deposited a sum of Rs.9,00,000/- but after realizing that as per the decree, he was liable to pay to the bank only a sum of Rs.7,56,832/- as on 31-12-2007, and that Rs.9,00,000/- deposited by him with the Bank was in 6 excess by Rs.1,43,168/- requested the Bank to refund the said excess amount. Since appellant - Bank failed to refund the excess amount, in spite of repeated requests, respondent has brought the suit for recovering the excess amount from the Bank. 6. The appellant-Bank after entering appearance in the suit, filed written statement admitting that it had filed a suit in O. S.117 /2000 against respondent for recovering Rs.8,46,828/- and during the pendency of the suit, respondent had paid a sum of Rs.4,55,500/-. The Court after giving deduction for the said payment, decreed the suit for Rs.3,91,328/- But it was contended that, since deduction was erroneous the appellant - Bank had filed an application with memo for amendment of the judgment and decree by showing the particulars of the amount and interest. The plaintiff had not filed any objection. 7 7. During the pendency of the earlier suit, the respondent had issued a request letter dt. 28-05-2004, which was marked as Ex.D.1 in the present suit to the Bank to settle the account under one time settlement scheme on remittance of Rs.9,00,000/- but respondent did not comply with that letter. Then during the pendency of the application filed by the Bank in O.S.No. 117/2000 for amendment of the judgment and decree passed in O. S.117 /2000, the Bank has issued a letter to withdraw the application as matter was settled for Rs.9,00,000/-. Hence, application filed for amendment of judgment and decree was not pressed. Therefore the respondent was estopped from filing the present suit. Further, respondent had not disputed the suit claim in O. S.117 /2000. In view of the compromise, the amendment application was not pressed. On this 8 ground, the Bank has prayed for dismissing the present suit. 8. The trial Court on the basis of the rival pleadings of the parties has framed the following issues for its consideration : 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that he was due Rs.7,56,832/- as on 31.12.2007 to the defendant as per the decree passed in O.S.No.117/2000? 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that he has paid excess amount of Rs.1,43,168/- to the defendant. If so, whether the plaintiff is entitled to recover the said amount? 3. Whether the defendant proves that the plaintiff has requested and offered to settle the matter out of Court for Rs.9,00,000/-? 4. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for reliefs as claimed for? 5. What order or decree? 9 9. Plaintiff - respondent in support of his case has examined himself as P.W.1 and has produced certified copy of the decree passed in O. S.117 /2000 which was marked as Ex.P.1. 10. On behalf of the appellant (defendant - Bank), the Bank Manager was examined as D.W.1, letter dt. 28-05-2004 addressed by the plaintiff to the Bank, order sheet of O. S.117 /2000 and I.A.No.2 filed by the Bank in O. S.117 /2000 and another letter, were marked as Exs. D.1 to D.4 respectively. 11. The trial Court after hearing the learned Counsel appearing for the parties and perusing the oral and documentary evidence on record has answered Issue No.1 and 2 in the affirmative and Issue No.3 in the negative and consequently decreed the suit of the plaintiff. 10 12. The defendant - Bank aggrieved by the judgment and decree of the trial Court has challenged the same by preferring R.A.17/2013 before the first appellate Court and it was also dismissed. It is challenging these concurrent judgments and decrees of the Courts below, the defendant - Bank has preferred this second appeal. 13. It is not in dispute that earlier the Bank had brought the suit in O. S.117 /2000 for recovering Rs.8,46,828/- from the present plaintiff - respondent. It is also not in dispute that during the pendency of O. S.117 /2000, the plaintiff in the present suit had paid a sum of Rs.4,56,500/- to the Bank. The trial Court taking the said payment into consideration and after giving deduction to the said amount decreed the suit of the Bank for a sum of Rs.3,91,328/- along with interest at 10% and cost of Rs.62,232/-. The principle 11 contention of the Bank was that the Court while giving deduction to Rs.4,55,500/- paid by the respondent, has committed an error in calculating the amount due to the Bank. The next contention of the Bank is, as there was error in the judgment and decree of the trial Court in O. S.117 /2000, an application for amendment of the judgment and decree was filed. Since respondent had agreed to pay Rs.9,00,000/- under OTS scheme, the application was not pressed. Except raising these contentions, the Bank which was in the custody of the correspondence made between the Bank and the respondent, did not produce any other documents to show as to whether matter was settled under OTS scheme for payment of Rs.9,00,000/-. The trial Court by taking into consideration of the fact that during the pendency of O. S.117 /2000 respondent had paid a sum of Rs.4,55,500/- and after giving deduction to the said payment has decreed the suit for Rs.3,91,328/- with 12 interest at 10% and costs. Accordingly as on 31-12-2007 respondent was liable to pay to the Bank a sum of Rs.7,56,832.19 ps. On the other hand, he had paid Rs.9,00,000/-. The Bank has not disputed that as on 31-12-2007, respondent was due to the Bank a sum of Rs.7,56,832/-. If the amount due to the Bank as on 31.12.2007 was Rs.7,56,832.19 ps., and if respondent had paid Rs.9,00,000/- on the said date i.e., - 31-12- 2007, obviously a sum of Rs.1,43,168/- would be excess. The trial Court considering this material aspect of the matter was justified in decreeing the suit of the respondent(plaintiff) and directing the appellant - Bank to refund the said excess amount to the respondent(plaintiff) with interest and cost. The lower appellate Court on reappreciation of entire oral and documentary evidence on record and having not found any error, irregularity or infirmity in the judgment and decree of the trial Court, dismissed the appeal. 13 14. I have carefully gone through the judgments and decrees passed by both the Courts below and do not find any error warranting my interference. That apart, there is absolutely no question of law, much less one urged in the appeal memo, warranting admission of the appeal. Accordingly, the appeal is dismissed as devoid of merits. In view of disposal of the appeal, I.A.No.1/2016 for stay does not survive for consideration and it is rejected. Sd/- JUDGE Mgn/-
